evidence: 217Ch calls 1A24h to compare current position state. If no printable span remains, 2182h-2189h clears EF3B and EF3E and jumps to the common EF42 update at 23E4h.
notes: This is the top of the geometry/output-template computation reached from 286Eh and 289Dh.

evidence: When VV87 bits 0+1 select the left-to-right path, 21EDh indexes by VV3A&07. 21F1h selects 730Fh table data, 21F7h indexes EE28 alignment correction, 2207h stores VV46, 2213h starts from EE66, and 2225h stores EF40.
notes: EF40 is derived from EE66 + table/correction terms + 3*EFB0 - 1. This path uses render geometry tables 730Fh, 7341h, 737Bh, and 738Bh.

evidence: When the normal/right-to-left path is taken, 22D7h indexes by VV3A&07. 22DBh selects 7307h table data for VV46, 22E4h uses 7317h, 22EBh starts from EE64, and 22FCh stores EF40 after subtracting 3*EFB6.
notes: This path uses render geometry tables 7307h, 7317h, 736Bh, and 735Bh. It is the sibling path to the left-to-right geometry computation.

evidence: 23C6h loads EFB4, 23CBh loads EFB2, 23D2h stores EF44 = EFB4 - EFB2. 23D6h uses VV3A&03 to select one byte from the table at 23E9h, then CALT ($009E) scales it before 23E4h stores EF42.
notes: Bytes at 23E9h are a dispatch/data table, not linear code. Current decoded bytes start 02 01 03 01 04 01 06 01; treat the table values as geometry scale selectors until the CALT ($009E) arithmetic is fully named.

evidence: 2864h clears VV38.3, 2867h calls 1A24h, 286Eh calls 217Ch when printable span remains, 2871h-2882h handles pending EE7A vertical motion, and 288Ah calls 5676h if VV36.0 indicates output is active.
notes: This is the immediate scheduler path that copies EF38..EF46 through 5676h. It is shared with paper-feed scheduling when EE7A is nonzero.

evidence: 289Dh calls 217Ch again, then 28A0h/28A3h update surrounding state. 28AAh can call 5676h again after VV36/VV37/VV85 busy-state checks. 28BAh can call 28CEh to queue the current template.
notes: This is the traced source of repeated print-output scheduling. Do not reduce it to "second pass = same glyph shifted" without following EF38..EF46 field changes.

evidence: 28CEh waits until VV82 != 04h, increments VV82, computes FFB0h + 15*VV83, copies 15 bytes from EF38..EF46 into that slot, then increments/wraps VV83 at five slots.
notes: The copied template later reaches live state through 08AAh-08C3h and 5679h. This queue is the trace-backed inter-run handoff.

evidence: 5676h sets HL=EF38 and falls into 5679h, which copies 15 bytes to EF6D..EF7B. The copied byte at source+2 becomes VV6F.
notes: External callers at 288Ah and 28AAh copy the current render-output template into the live scheduler. VV6F later controls count halving and carriage timing record selection.

evidence: 563Ch clears/sets VV6D flags, ORs VV6F with 04h, loads EF64, halves it at 5650h only when VV6F.1 is set, stores the result to EF79, and saves alternate-register DE and HL-derived values into EF75 and EF77.
notes: This proves multi-pass count scaling and source/count capture. It does not identify the logical image-buffer phase of the captured source.

evidence: 08D0h writes F004=40h or C0h according to VV61.0. 08DBh-08E8h loads alternate DE from EF75/EF77. 08EAh-08F5h reloads EF64 from EF79, doubling it first when VV61.1 is set.
notes: The head ISR consumes this alternate-register state; direction affects both F004 command and DE increment/decrement order.

evidence: 0978h enters alternate registers and emits exactly three F005 bytes per interrupt. With VV61.0 clear it reads DE+; with VV61.0 set it reads DE-. 099Dh decrements the burst counter, 09ACh-09BCh cycles TM1 timing, and 09A2h-09ABh terminates the burst.
notes: The output stream is contiguous in the chosen direction from the captured source pointer. Any LQ pass-phase model must explain how EF75/EF77 point at the intended stream.

evidence: The trace proves 360-DPI LQ glyph columns, render-output geometry setup, repeated/queued scheduler templates, count halving for VV6F.1, and contiguous three-byte head bursts. It does not yet prove that the second LQ pass blindly replays the same logical columns shifted by one dot.
notes: Next trace target: decode EF38..EF46 field meanings across successive 217Ch calls and through 563Ch's EF75/EF77 capture, using ROM state rather than PDF visual approximation.
